ew_context: Inverse H&S pattern suggests completion of corrective decline and resumption of uptrend from key support zone
pattern_desc: Well-formed inverse head and shoulders with head at 90.69 and neckline at 94.24. Right shoulder testing support above head low confirms pattern validity.
invalidation: Close below 92.00 on D1 timeframe would break the right shoulder and negate the bullish reversal pattern
market_context: Risk-on sentiment supporting JPY crosses despite broader macro uncertainty. NZD strength on China PMI expansion data
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

NZDJPY has carved out a textbook inverse head and shoulders pattern with the head formed at 90.69 and neckline resistance at 94.24. The right shoulder at current levels around 93.73 is holding well above the head low, confirming pattern integrity. This reversal structure suggests the corrective decline from recent highs has completed and uptrend resumption is likely.

RSI at 56.9% shows healthy momentum without overbought conditions, while EMA20 at 93.36 above EMA50 at 92.86 confirms bullish trend alignment. ATR of 0.67 provides reasonable volatility for the 2.7:1 risk-reward setup. Laminar flow with momentum alignment supports the bullish thesis.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 94.24 (critical neckline breakout level), 97.80 (measured pattern target)
- **Support**: 92.19 (pattern invalidation and stop protection)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 92.00 on D1 timeframe would break the right shoulder structure and negate the bullish reversal pattern completely.

## Macro & Flow Context

China's PMI expansion supports risk sentiment benefiting NZD, while JPY weakness on carry trade dynamics favors the cross. Pattern completion aligns with broader risk-on flows despite geopolitical tensions.
